title: WQO — Tool Type Entry
description: Schema for entries in the WQO tool-type taxonomy. A tool type is a category of instrument, method, or infrastructure used in document production. Entries span all production eras and media. Named products and frameworks appear as non-normative examples only.
x-note: stage values align with D7 writing_stage in concept.schema.json. persona values align with D12 responsible_role. category groups tool types by nature of the instrument.

id.description: Canonical kebab-case identifier, e.g. linter, style-guide, typewriter.

name.description: Display name of the tool type.

category.description: Nature of the instrument. digital-software=applications and automated tools; method-framework=structured practices, standards, and guidelines; physical-analog=material tools and pre-digital technologies; platform-infrastructure=hosting, collaboration, and coordination environments.

description.description: One to two sentences describing what this tool type does and when it is relevant in document production.

stage.description: Writing stage(s) at which this tool type is characteristically active. Aligns with D7 writing_stage. Omit if the tool type spans all stages or the affinity is not meaningful.

persona.description: Production role(s) that characteristically use this tool type. Aligns with D12 responsible_role. Omit or use empty array if the tool type is role-agnostic.

examples.description: Non-normative illustrative instances — named products, specific frameworks, or historical artefacts. Not exhaustive.

also_known_as.description: Informal names, discipline-specific synonyms, or alternate terms for this tool type.

notes.description: Optional clarifying remarks, scope boundaries, or distinctions from similar tool types.

writingStageEnum.description: Aligns with D7 writing_stage in concept.schema.json.

personaEnum.description: Aligns with D12 responsible_role in concept.schema.json (ai-tool excluded — it is a tool type, not a persona).
